[00:30]

[00:40]
"Uh, one thing I do want to say is that you all have a unfair advantage in this room. I actually wish I knew a little bit of marketing before I started working on YouTube because you all understand a certain level of EQ, working with audiences all day, running ads, and everything that most creators when they start YouTube, they're not thinking about that."
[01:00]
"They're just like, 'I want to document my life.' And it's like, 'All right, cool. No one cares.'"
[01:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Disclaimers"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Slide introdutório com pontos sobre vantagens de marketers, velocidade da apresentação, contexto situacional
[01:10]
"Uh, last disclaimer is it just depends. There's a lot of stuff on YouTube where people will ask me questions and I have to ask like seven follow-up questions before we get to the real answer. So, a lot of stuff you'll see may apply to you, may not apply to you, and it just depends very situationally."
[01:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Myths to Dispel"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Lista de mitos comuns sobre YouTube: CTR perfeito, comprimento de vídeo, keywords, "just another filming day", comprar views/subs
[01:35]
"So, a few myths that I just want to dispel right off the bat. People ask me a lot, Gabe, what's the perfect click-through rate? There is no perfect click-through rate at all whatsoever. It is totally dependent on your baselines for the channel."
[02:00]
"So, what I always say is like, if I gave you a quote unquote perfect click-through rate, it wouldn't matter for certain people more than others. Like, let's say you were a brand new vlogger channel, you may have like 20 people watch your video and your video got, you know, 40 impressions. You have a 50% clickthrough rate."
[02:20]
"Are you going to go out and be like, I have a 50% clickthrough rate? It's like, probably not. You don't have enough statistical significance. So, it really depends."
[02:30]
"Uh video length. So, I heard it earlier. I think it was from you, Nick. It's like, well, how long should my videos be? It's like make them as long as you can make them valuable. There is no right way."
[02:45]
"Keywords. Keywords to me is like looking at the disclaimer that your that your funnel is not associated with Facebook on the bottom of the footer. Keywords do not matter whatsoever. Do not get looped into like tools about YouTube that talk about keywords."
[03:00]
"They literally have even the people who run the algorithm say keywords don't matter. So you could put like some like your name if your name is commonly misspelled maybe, but I'm usually like tags and keywords. I'm putting like five and then I'm just moving on. Sometimes I don't even upload them."
[03:20]
"This one is this one's really big. Just another filming day. So I hear a lot of people like, 'Oh, I got to just film. Oh, two hour just film.' I'm like, a lot of the top creators are not treating their filming days like this. They are taking it much more seriously."
[03:40]
"So how I want you to really think about this is if your mindset going into a film day is, 'Oh, I'm just going to film another 20 videos. Oh, my content team, my VA team gave me another 40.' It's like that's just going to be another video that somebody's just going to scroll past because that's how you're treating it."
[04:00]
"A lot of the top creators put a lot a lot of time into their videos. Uh this one's my favorite. Should I just buy subs, views, and watch time? I swear to god, it's from a legit company. It's like I'm going to show you really bad examples of what ends up happening and how the algorithm penalizes you pretty much just destroy your channel right off the bat."
[04:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Core Beliefs About YouTube"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Lista de crenças fundamentais: melhor plataforma para reputação, sempre broad targeting, earning vs owed distribution, persistência vs consistência, micro 1% improvements
[04:25]
"Cool. So core beliefs that have served me very very well about when I think about YouTube. Number one is I truly believe that YouTube maybe LinkedIn because Omar kind of convinced me today on his bombass presentation by the way. I think it's one of the best platforms to increase the value and reputation that you have in the marketplace for people to transact with you."
[04:50]
"If you're thinking about all the content that you're putting out and the algorithm is serving to audiences and new audiences all the time, that's reciprocity that you now have at scale. YouTube is all broad. There is no targeting aside from the video that you make. So essentially, you are always broad targeting with your videos."
[05:10]
"The other thing is really important distinction. This frames people a lot for a much longer time horizon which I'll talk about as well. You are earning a distribution channel. That is way different than saying, 'Oh, I'm going to upload videos and then like what's happening? What's my ROI in one month?' That's not the game that you're playing here."
[05:35]
"You are. And there's a clear distinction. You're earning a distribution channel. It's not owed to you just because you make content. Persistence versus consistence. Micro 1% along the way is everything."
[05:50]
"I think that's why this slideshow is over 200 slides because I really have to solidify it's a lot of just 1% things that you're doing over and over because I could make a different type of presentation where it's like well just make your content better. It's like but what does that actually mean? How do you quote unquote make your content more valuable? Things like that."
[06:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Channel Growth Timeline Examples"
NÚMEROS: 3 meses, 6 meses, 2 anos
VISUAL: Gráficos mostrando crescimento de canais ao longo do tempo: primeiro exemplo mostra crescimento explosivo após 3 meses, segundo exemplo mostra canal morto revivendo após 6 meses
[06:15]
"YouTube gives you a lot of data to help you push over the end. The other thing is persistence versus consistence. A lot of people say, 'Oh, well I just have to post three times a day and I'm just remaining consistent. Remaining consistent.' I actually don't really like that too much."
[06:35]
"It's a lot about being persistent in the certain metrics that you're measuring versus just posting for the sake of posting because that's that's literally what you're doing."
[06:45]
"So, just to give you like a little bit better of a time horizon for some of the channels I worked on, the first three months was kind of like this until this person saw explosive growth. So, that's like one mindset that this person, you know, you're doing the content game, you're doing you're doing it and sometimes it takes three months, sometimes it takes six months."
[07:10]
"This other person, right? This is a channel that like died and is trying to come back to life. So, six months, right? Took them. They finally spiked. They were getting pretty good traffic."
[07:25]
"But remember, since they earned their distribution channel now, when they start to release videos, they still have a bit of that audience that's willing to listen to them, even though they haven't released a video for like two years."
[07:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Raise the Floor, Not Push the Ceiling"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito visual mostrando diferença entre viral hits (teto) vs crescimento consistente do baseline (chão)
[07:45]
"Next belief that has served me very well is you're always trying to push the ceiling. Sorry, you're trying to raise the floor, not push the ceiling. So your video may go viral, but that doesn't mean that your channel overall is growing. What you want to be doing is those 1% consistent raising the floor over time versus trying to go for those viral hits that really don't serve you most of the time."
[08:10]
"So this is I was like astounded. I told Jeremy it's like such a great lineup is I think if you were really intent on listening to everyone you hear them talk Chris Brandon and Nick on a much much longer time horizon."
[08:25]
"Like if you notice when Nick was I'm sorry when Chris was talking he spoke about four years. I'm going to do this for four years. Not oh one month. Where's my ROI? It's like he's thinking totally different."
[08:40]
"Brandon when he teaches his students and when he was talking he was talking about content in a two-year time horizon or with the podcast you listen to him he's like oh yeah in a few years when I have this popping podcast right same thing with Nick like super long term right."
[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Attention Spans Myth"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Desconstrução do mito de attention span curta, referência à indústria cinematográfica, conceito de conteúdo boring vs valioso
[09:05]
"People don't really have shorter attention spans I really or like quote unquote prefer shorter content. I I really dislike this kind of like humans have a goldfish kind of experience blah blah you're just like very boring because you're essentially telling me that the movie industry is dead which it's not."
[09:25]
"So really I just think about it as like your content is too boring slash like you just got to put your ego in check. It's like you're just not offering enough value to the marketplace where people are keeping attention."
[09:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "YouTube Algorithms Overview"
NÚMEROS: 90%+ das views vêm de Browse + Suggested
VISUAL: Diagrama mostrando 4 tipos de algoritmos: Browse (recommendation/subscribers), Suggested (homepage/up next), Search, External
[09:45]
"Cool. So the algorithm like I wish I had a mist machine. A lot of people want to know about this but I'm going to give you what you want to actually show you what you need. Algorithm is actually like not really that important in a way audiences but we're going to cover algorithm just so everybody can like oh yeah but what about the algorithm so let's just cover it real quick."
[10:10]
"Number one algorithm is browse. This is for recommendation/subscribers. So like on mobile it's your home and subscription feeds where people are subscribed to you slash they're discovering new content."
[10:25]
"The second one very similar is suggested. So that's YouTube homepage. Once again that's like up next or if you have autoplay on your videos playing after another video and that only shows up on the home screen. These two combined is about more than 90% of views that happen on YouTube come from these two algorithms."
[10:50]
"So these are the ones that you want to optimize potentially for. If you have like an affiliate channel, you're probably doing more search than anything else, but that's a little little different. So obviously you have the search algorithm that's using a different one."
[11:10]
"And then you have external, which is like got really tiny external, which YouTube can't track. So a lot of times that's what happens with bot farms and things like this."
[11:20]
"When you have external traffic and we're talking about the algorithm. When you sign up for like getting views and bot farms, things like that, they have like a long list of VAS in a tier three country that are clicking on random ass videos and then going to yours."
[11:40]
"So, like if you think about what the algorithm is essentially meant to do, and I'll talk about it, it's serving a cat video or like some Minecraft video that a kid's mom paid for to make them feel better and then it's showing your video and it's making that association and YouTube sees it and it can't really track what's happening in between those because there's no coherent reason why somebody's watching a business video and then a Minecraft video."
[12:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How YouTube Algorithm Actually Works"
NÚMEROS: Bilhões de minutos de vídeo por segundo
VISUAL: Fluxograma técnico mostrando: Container de vídeos → Seleção para viewer → Ranking → Feed principal. Processo de filtragem em múltiplas etapas
[12:15]
"Cool. So long time ago, I didn't know that LinkedIn actually did this, Omar. But YouTube, the engineers at YouTube released how the algorithm actually worked. It's very complicated. They released like a four-page paper with a whole bunch of fancy language that I wish I had chat GPT back in the day to teach me what they were talking about."
[12:40]
"So I'll break it down very very easily. So all videos go into container. There's like billions of video minutes getting uploaded to YouTube per second, by the way. So they need a way of filtering that. The first way of filtering that is selecting videos for a viewer."
[13:00]
"This is probably like this. I'm going to emphasize this point probably the most. This is the number one thing that is different than Facebook. With YouTube, if you think about a stream of like a stream of consciousness for viewers, we're used to running ads where like we're putting money to put videos in front of people. That is not what YouTube is doing."
[13:25]
"Like you think they are, but that's that's totally opposite. What they're doing is like think about a audience of viewers. It's using a claw machine to get your video and pull it up into the stream. That's way different than putting money to try to get it."
[13:45]
"So this is all about optimizing and finding the right video for the right viewer. Then of course it's doing rankings. So once again, it's just keep filtering like it's ranking you with other people."
[14:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Algorithm Selection Factors"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Lista detalhada de fatores: histórico do usuário, contexto, idade, gênero, feedback do vídeo, notificações, fontes externas
[14:05]
"I'm not going to go through everything on this checklist. Like I said, there's one of the slides that you can view later, but it's using history and context. What have they watched before? What have they engaged with? uh age, gender, blah blah blah."
[14:20]
"Then it's using video feedback. So like when you say like this video, right? Never say that in your video, but use a use a better call to action. Like make it funny. It's using video feedback on platform such as like if you get YouTube notifications for a creator, if they're clicking into the app, uh things like that."
[14:45]
"Then it's using other sources. The reason this one is also in red is because this is also what happens with bot forms because it's looking at how did they get to the video and if Google has any data on that user since you're in their ecosystem."
[15:05]
"And then of course you have you just have your main feed and that's where people are viewing your your content either in their subscription feed, search feed, blah blah blah blah. Now keep in mind YouTube's doing all of this for just one viewer. So it does all of that just for one. And when it builds off of more than one, what do we call that? An audience."
[15:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "YouTube Serves Audiences - TAM/Niche/Focus Framework"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Diagrama em camadas mostrando: TAM (Total Addressable Market - Business content) → Niche (Marketing for business owners) → Focus (Party of one - domínio específico)
[15:35]
"Cool. YouTube serves audiences. Like I was saying, it does all of that to pick your video to put into like a stream of videos of consciousness for a specific audience. So a lot of the times when I'm thinking about YouTube audiences and people are like, 'Oh, what kind of videos should I make?' Things like that, I really think about the the TAM of what you're talking about."
[16:00]
"So, I'll use an example for a business. For example, since most business owners here, if not everyone, let's say our whole TAM is people who are interested in business content, right? Now, think about you're in a big ass stream with a lot of people producing videos."
[16:20]
"When you're first starting your channel, I would recommend against going super super wide. Then you have niche. So, it's like you're a business owner teaching what? You're you're teaching let's say marketing. Cool. So, you're you're you're a business owner teaching marketing to business owners, agency owners, chiropractors, like what what are you doing?"
[16:45]
"Then you have focus. And this is where a lot of people really get messed up. For focus, you really want to be like a party of just one. Like people go to you for that very specific thing. Like Chris is a once again really good example of this for Omar. It's like he's dominated that focus."
[17:10]
"Then he can make niche content. People trust him a lot more. He has earned the right to move up in an audience because he has dominated his focus of just that."
[17:25]
"And a lot of people think, oh, well, it's like might get saturated, things like that, blah, blah, blah, blah. It's it's not really about that. You have to really focus because YouTube is judging how well you do with people who are your most raging fans and can you multiply your raging fans to grow an audience into higher tam."
[17:50]
"So, a lot of ways to look at this too, and I'll talk about it. Whenever you're like, I want to get more views, you just have to think about right now I'm focused on this. How do I move up my niche and then how do I move into TAM?"
[18:10]
"If you think about like Mr. Beast has, who's watched Mr. Beast video here? All right, cool. If you want to be on YouTube, you should definitely watch like five to 10 of his videos because you're looking at the most successful creator on the platform essentially."
[18:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Algorithm Is Not The Solution"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Mensagem central: quando vídeos não performam, analise a audiência e o conteúdo, não culpe o algoritmo
[18:35]
"Cool. Uh, once again, like I was talking about earlier, the algorithm is not the it's not the solution. So, you always want to think about the videos when they don't do well. What didn't go well with the audience and what your video did for them. It's not an algorithm explanation."
[18:55]
"The I want you to really think about this. Like for the algorithm, is it like more likely that the algorithm that serves right videos to billions of people selected you as the person that it's like, 'No, we're going to this person today.' It's like, probably not."
[19:15]
"It's they probably didn't do that. it's most likely that your video is trash and you need to put your ego away and really look at like why didn't this resonate with my audience? Was it my hook? Was it the story I was telling? Was it the content? Like really, really think about it."
[19:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Horse Dentist Case Study"
NÚMEROS: 700K views = $0 sales, 5.4K views = $30-50K sales
VISUAL: Comparação de dois vídeos: viral broad content vs conteúdo específico whale, demonstração dramática de diferença de ROI
[19:40]
"Um, and then once again, why going broad to get views? Dopamine is usually not the best thing to do on YouTube. I'll tell you a quick story. So, I was consulting with a horse dentist. By a show of hands, does anybody know of a horse dentist? Oh my gosh, you're the You're ruining my joke a little bit, Omar."
[20:05]
"So, horse dentists are very rare. They're a very rare breed. So, I was working with this horse dentist. He was like, I want to get views like Mr. Beast. I was like, yikes where that's a wrong expectation for that qualifying call that I took, but I was like, you know what? Yeah, sure. Like, we'll we'll think about it."
[20:30]
"So, there was a lot of content in between this like so I kind of summarized it because, you know, I I want to show everyone the example. So we identified like what are other adjacent trends in the niche because I have to move up from his focus and his niche to a TAM thing."
[20:50]
"So like what are things that happen with animals that people gravitate towards that get millions of views? It's usually like them abandoned or like some happening to some innocent animal or being abused."
[21:05]
"So then basically we made this video. This video and then this one is like huge dental infection in young rescued horse. It's like, yeah, most people probably watch that because it's a young innocent little horse and they're like, 'What the hell's going on with the little horse?' And that that video got 700k views. Zero sales. Zero."
[21:35]
"So, I want you to keep in mind this person teaches other veterinarians how to do horse dentistry. Zero views. He was like, 'What? But I have so many.' Okay. Well, dude, you wanted to do that."
[21:50]
"So, then next we were like, you know what? Let's release a video that's like only targets this weird ass language. You know, it's like E O T R H Diagnostics and Treatment. And you would think like, oh, you wouldn't you wouldn't entitle a video like that, but the issue is that his demographic of people who he services are looking for that specific thing."
[22:20]
"And he's the only one, I mean, I wouldn't say only, but he was the only one with the video to actually make something like that. So, when a ranch owner comes to a local veterinarian and they're like, 'My horse has this weird problem that Gabe doesn't know how to pronounce.' They watch this video and they're like, 'Oh crap, this guy definitely like I have to hire him to help me through this.'"
[22:50]
"And that video made like 30 to 50k for him through book calls. It's pretty wild. Uh 5.4K, a whooping 5.4K."
[23:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Dan Lok Channel Strategy - Video Intent"
NÚMEROS: 2.5K (preço HTC na época)
VISUAL: Grid mostrando diferentes tipos de vídeo com intenções específicas: pitch high ticket closer, pitch copywriter, lifestyle, belief shift, test (interview video que se tornou #1)
[23:10]
"So, when I was working on Dan's channel, another thing is having really high intention for the video going out. So a lot of things that people saw is like, 'Oh, Dan's producing a lot of content.' But make no mistake, we were very strategic on every single video that went out."
[23:30]
"Every single video had an intent on why we were doing it. It wasn't just like random stuff. So it was like, 'Oh, we're pitching high ticket closer. We're pitching high- income copywriter. This is a lifestyle.' A lot of belief shift content that like pushes them to believe a certain thing to prime them to be more receptive to when we pitch them in the YouTube channel."
[24:00]
"And then the other really cool one is the test one actually became it literally was the most random thing that we thought about. I was like, 'Yo, Dan, you kind of have like a lot of people don't speak English on your channel. Maybe you should talk about like speaking English and like getting job interviews because they may be like lower income, not even being able to afford HTC, which was like 2.5K at the time.'"
[24:30]
"So then he just made that video and that's actually the number one video now that eclipses the sales videos that he used to do. The how to interview. It's totally different. He doesn't pitch anything in that video. It's just him talking about how to interview better. It was like it was a 10% test."
[24:55]
"So I always say like you'll see it in the the thing I talk about later, but always just test and those wild tests might work. It's just like cool and you're expect you but it's very clear you go in with the expectation that this shit's probably going to bomb."
[25:15]
"Like you don't go in saying this is going to be a viral video. It's like no way. But ended up being the most viral video. It's pretty crazy."
[25:25]
"So once again, when you're having a lot of that conversation, save the banger videos and pitch in those videos. Like don't make the mistake of being I'm going to create a new topic and we're going to launch this new thing and pitch it. It's like no. Use the content that already works and then just pitch in that video because you already know it works. That's it."
[25:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pitch in Proven Winners"
NÚMEROS: 100K+ views
VISUAL: Exemplo de vídeos sobre "millionaire/rich" que consistentemente ultrapassam 100K views - esses são os vídeos onde fazer pitch
[25:55]
"Uh yeah, of course. So for one of these, right, we already know that when we talk on Dan's channel about millionaire or rich, it gets over 100k views. So we're going to pitch in that video versus concepts."
[26:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Whales vs Guppies Content"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito visual de conteúdo whale (high-value audience) vs guppy (broad views), metáfora de usar isca errada para peixe errado
[26:20]
"Uh yeah. Concepts and teams on your channel. So, whales versus guppies. So, a little bit about the horse dentistry type of client, things like that. A lot of times what I see is that people are doing very generic stuff and they're appeasing to guppy content for views versus whale content, right?"
[26:45]
"I think Omar actually talked about you. You said whale in your presentation. I'm like, yo, we're like linked, bro. That's why I with Omar, you know? It's cool. So, you're using the wrong bait. So a lot of times because you don't really necessarily want the views, you just want the right audience and you want YouTube specifically to make the association that you want that audience."
[27:15]
"You don't want them thinking that you want a lot of views and random people on their phones are just watching your channel. And sometimes, like I said, this is less views. Like that guy got 5.4K, made 30 to 50K off that one video versus his 700K video made zero sales. I think he had like one book. It was like no show. It's kind of funny."
[27:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Guppy vs Whale Content Examples"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Quiz interativo mostrando títulos de vídeo e classificando como guppy (broad appeal) ou whale (specific high-value audience)
[27:45]
"Cool. Let's play a quick game. We're going to talk about guppies versus whale content. So, I'm going to create a video from poor to rich in 18 months. Is this a whale or a guppy content? Great job. Going from one-on-one to group coaching. I've spoiled it. Whale."
[28:10]
"How to make 10K a month. Agency for sure. Frameworks to make your clients more and double your revenue. Oh, we got we got that was a mix. Steps to take your first agency deal. Yep. Exactly."
[28:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Whale Content Question Framework"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados

[28:35]

Like, a lot of those questions, gear yourself to thinking about just targeting those because they're very specific to like your specific perfect client avatar."
[29:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Continuum - 1% Method"
NÚMEROS: 1%
VISUAL: Framework mostrando: 1% (repetir ações de sucesso) → Inovação (criar novo conteúdo) → Externo (inspiração de outras indústrias)
[29:05]
"So, let's talk a little bit about content continuum. Gabe, what content should I make? How do I think about like my content calendar? Things like that. The first thing I do is called 1%. I think everyone should really listen to Jeremy. We learned it from Grant. learned it from who knows where. Repeat successful actions."
[29:30]
"This is the top 1% of stuff that's working on your channel. Just just straight up repeat it. A lot of people get into this weird really weird thing. They're like, 'But but I already made that video.' It's like, 'I'm sorry. Does that video have all of the views of your subscribers and your returning viewer rate is 100%.' No, I didn't think so because I've never seen it and I've analyzed like tons of YouTube channels. So, just remake it."
[30:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "1% Metrics to Track"
NÚMEROS: Subs/New Views, Average % Viewed, New Viewers/Views, Average Views per Viewer
VISUAL: Tabela de métricas diferentes para identificar vídeos top 1% em cada categoria específica
[30:05]
"Some quick things. I'm going to go through this really, really fast. We just do subs to new views. I have something at the end that'll help you with this. So, all for free, things like that. But basically, we're just measuring how many subscribers gained by new viewers to see if that content is actually growing."
[30:25]
"Same thing with this. It's just average percentage viewed. How long are people watching that are new? Same thing new viewers divided by views. Like, how many of those views are new viewers? And these are all different videos if you notice."
[30:45]
"And it's just different videos that are 1% in that category. And that's how you choose to move that lever for that 1% change on the channel. Other one. This one's average views per viewer. This is how many times the same person is coming back to a video."
[31:05]
"That's really good signals for the algorithm because they're like, 'Damn, this video is so good that somebody actually came back and watched this.' It's pretty wild."
[31:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Export Analytics Data"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Instruções para exportar dados: Advanced Analytics → Três pontos no canto superior direito → Export to Google Sheets
[31:25]
"Cool. How do you get this data? You just go into your advanced analytics. You click a dot dot dot on the top right and then export into Google Sheets. And there it is."
[31:40]
"So, next up, you have you think it's really important for a lot of people in this room, a lot of creators, you know, also expand the bounds of innovation. Don't get stuck just being all analytical all the time. just like create content that you want to talk about as well."
[32:00]
"Like that's totally okay. Like you have new thoughts on certain things that have happened in your business or situations. Just talk about it, right? I mean, I don't mean it like just talk about get in vlog and like have no attention editing, but think about it. Like stuff you want to create is fine."
[32:20]
"A lot of videos when I used to work with Dan and other creators, they're just like they just want to talk about it and we just have to frame it properly and think about like does the audience actually want to know about this as well? And you make trade-offs."
[32:40]
"Like there's a series on Dan's channel of him unboxing Hermes bags with his wife. I'm like I hate this I didn't want to release it at all. He's like oh but it's like my brand. I'm like I get it but damn those videos sucked audience."
[33:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Sources: Related Channels & External"

[33:05]
"So remember I was talking about the stream of consciousness. YouTube actually gives you what the stream of consciousness and how they pair your videos with the stream of consciousness does as well."
[33:20]
"So, in your analytics, you could do this on mobile, too, but in your analytics, you'll see it'll show you what other channels your audience watches and then also what videos they watch as well, like very specifically what videos or shorts they're watching as well."
[33:40]
"So, what I like to do is when I start working with a creator, I'll look at these channels, of course, see like what type of content they're doing, things like that. But I also will like take their most recent titles and thumbnails and like put them on this channel. I'm like, would somebody click mine over the other creators or the other creators videos? Like, how how like truly how good is my content compared to everybody else?"
[34:10]
"Then we have external. So external is like stuff that may be working in a different industry. This is like the 10% stuff. So the remember the how to like answer for an interview? We just saw that going viral somewhere else. It got like two million views and I was like, 'Yo, yeah, Dan, like your audience is like all bilingual in a way. They don't really speak that well. So like why don't you just teach them because he kind of teaches like English in that video too. So it's like a little bit of a hybrid."
[34:45]
"So this is one of the JVs that we used to do. His name was Improvement Pill does like whiteboard videos. So then we just made like a whiteboard video for Dan and it's 103K views and we got some sales from that to be fair. But it was higher than our baseline at that time. I think our baseline at that time was around like 63K on average for the first, you know, couple days."
[35:15]
"So it's just like take inspiration and like really just see how you could apply it to yours. And sometimes these things really do take off."
[35:25]
"Once again, towards the end, I'll kind of give you one of the best tricks too that I use to come up with content. Quick hint, it's not chat GBT. I'm not going to talk about AI because that's a totally different presentation. But everything you do here, you could ask ChatGBT to just answer these questions for you."
[35:50]
"And the trick that I like to do is like give it the framework that I have where it's like the TAM niche and focus and ask it to like ideulate based off of that. Like how would this video look like if it was super specific? If I wanted to appease the most people or appeal to the most people, what would my title be? And just like edit from there. Don't copy and paste from chat GPT, please."
[36:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "3Q Review Process"
NÚMEROS: 100 (número arbitrário de início), 3 etapas
VISUAL: Framework simples: Quantity (100 vídeos) → Quantify (medir métricas) → Quality (fazer 1 versão excelente)
[36:25]
"Uh, okay. So, talking about quantity now and how do we get through that? So, I heard a lot of conversations about quality versus quantity. How much should I post? Things like that. So, I'll just give you very quick how I think about things. This is how I run all the channels. 3Q review."
[36:45]
"First thing we go for is quantity. So, we're just 100. This is a arbitrary number. It could be 20. You're just like filtering stuff down. So just go for straight quantity first. Then you're just quantifying using certain YouTube metrics to see what's actually working. What is your audience resonating with? And then you're just making one really good quality version of that."
[37:15]
"Like it's not complicated. Like guys, this is really really not complicated. Like it's really simple framework, but a lot of people just don't do it and they overcomplicate the hell out of it."
[37:30]
"So the other thing is that right now I'm just talking about videos but really expert YouTube creators operate like an Olympic athlete as I represented here. They're doing multiple things all at once in different things of the video. So we talked about the amount of videos you're doing but this may be for them how are the locations that they're shooting in videos. How are like how fast are they talking? How slow are they talking? What are they talking about? Things like that."
[38:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Long Form vs Short Form"
NÚMEROS: $1,000 = 1 hora de conteúdo
VISUAL: Fórmula de Dan: para cada $1,000 que você quer que gastem, eles devem consumir 1 hora do seu conteúdo. Short = "I like what you did", Long = "I like who you are"
[38:05]
"Big question as well. Long form versus short form. Big big hot debate. So, we heard this yesterday from Brandon who learned it from Dan. That's who I learned it from, too, who learned it from who knows what, but it sounded really good because Dan made a lot of money."
[38:25]
"So, for every $1,000 spent that you want them to spend with you, they consume at least one hour of your content. This is like my favorite for sales teams, by the way, because when people get on sales calls from YouTube leads, I think you probably Nick could answer this one, but it's like when you get YouTube leads on the phone, just way easier. Yeah. Super indoctrinated. Yeah. They're they're they're the best ones."
[38:55]
"So, I always think about it like this. Short form is like, I like what you did there. Long form is I like who you are. So, I'm always opting for both. But I definitely lean, to be honest, I lean way more on long form because a lot of indoctrination happens on long form and people grow to know, like, and trust you versus it's very easy to just be, 'Oh, yeah, that was cool.' and then swipe to the next one."
[39:25]
"Versus long form, usually for myself personally, if I watch a creator's long form, I'm like, 'Oh, this is actually pretty cool.' Like, I'll check out their channel. If I watch one short that I like, I usually like I'm not going to the creator and like pimping out and binging."
[39:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Long Form Story Structure"
NÚMEROS: 5 segundos (hook inicial)
VISUAL: Framework de estrutura narrativa: Hook (5s) → Open Loop → Rehook → Open Loop → Payoff → Hook/Open Loop (próximo vídeo)
[39:50]
"So, best story and long form tips that I have. This is once again I'm just explaining a lot of and I'm sure because you guys are all very sophisticated marketers. A lot of this stuff is just doing very basic stuff just at very high levels and like really paying attention to it. It's just a story of open loops."
[40:10]
"This is how I kind of look at every single video when I'm scripting it with the creator. We start with the hook. That's the first 5 seconds. Very similar to ads. Like what is the hook? Why are they watching? Do not start your video. Hey, welcome to my channel. Oh my gosh. If I see that for you guys, I really I'm going to go in and edit it out of your own YouTube channel. Like it's it's terrible. Or ask for subscribers. Hey, welcome. Please like, subscribe. It helps the algorithm. Oh my gosh, you are asking for trouble."
[40:45]
"Then you have open loop. So that's just like open loop them. Like a lot in people's videos, they'll like have a hook, but then they'll wait to like do a payoff or something. It's like open loop them. Give them another question. Pop up something of curiosity that might happen later on in the video or that you'll reveal."
[41:10]
"It may be something like, 'Hey, these are the seven tips and I think you'll be really surprised with number four.' Blah, blah, blah. That could also be a hook to be fair. Then you're just rehooking, reminding them why they're staying for the video. You're open looping them again."
[41:30]
"Once again, it's just a compression of how much you could either do this a lot in a in a video or, you know, space it out."
[41:40]
"Reooping is like reminding them. Exactly. Reminding them why they clicked on this video in the first place versus an open loop. It's more like a question you'll probably answer in the video, but it's just keeping them for that micro micro moment."
[42:00]
"Then you have the payoff. Very important for payoff. How you do this open loop right here matters for what's going to happen at the end of the video because this open loop I like to set up what's going to happen after the payoff because a lot of people you do seven tips. As soon as you do the seventh tip, like people are leaving that video."
[42:25]
"Like I have way too much data to show. like as soon as you give them the reward and you don't set them up for anything after you're you're in a world of hurt and then your drop off rate comes retention blah blah."
[42:40]
"So then the last one is hook/open loop. I put both there because you could do and what you're doing essentially is setting up for the next video. So when I talk about up next I'm saying this part is so short it's like me saying uh let me think."
[43:00]
"So hook, open loop. It's like saying, 'Hey, if you like this YouTube talk, I actually was going to run out of time, so I left out two other things that's in this video.' Boom. Your video is done. There is no like, 'Oh, thank you. And actually, you know, let's take a picture. Here's my wife. Subscribe if you like this video. Really helps me.' Holy Please, please, please, please, don't do that."
[43:30]
"Or or do a call to action at the very end. Like, if you like this video, then check out my other content. And if you want to work with me, click on the link below. It's like, please don't do that, guys. It kind of look pretty spammy to be honest."
[43:50]
"And once again, if you're doing longer videos, just compress this. Like you're just compressing this into uh a shorter time frame."
[44:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Retention = Attention"
NÚMEROS: 25% vs 68% retention (exemplo comparativo)
VISUAL: Conceito de que baixa retention prejudica seleção do algoritmo, YouTube otimiza para session time total
[44:05]
"Cool. Let's talk about retention a little bit. So if you're low, if your videos have lower retention, this is something that I always consider. Low retention equals attention. YouTube does not like you to have low retention. Its whole purpose is to increase what we call session time."
[44:30]
"It's not a metric you can actually measure on YouTube, unfortunately, but what they're measuring is the whole time that you kept somebody on the YouTube platform. So, if you have low retention, think about the stream of consciousness."
[44:50]
"Remember, YouTube is not you're not paying money for YouTube to promote your content. It's selecting it like a claw machine into the stream of consciousness. When it's doing that, let's say I'm competing with a video that Jeremy made. My video is at 25% retention. Jeremy's a baller at content for sure. He has 68% retention."
[45:15]
"Now, algorithm is going to come and look. Oh, look at these little Oh, it's like, oh, you know what? this this person is going to keep 68% of people interested and on YouTube versus Gab's 25%. It doesn't look very good. They're trying to increase their ad revenue and people on the platform. So, they will choose Jeremy's video over mine. Retention is really, really big deal."

[46:10]

So text usually has spikes in retention. So just a good tip. Same thing, you'll see it there. It's like one of the quick hacks, especially if it's like long people pause. They like like to take screenshots or if you show them like exact frameworks, things like that. People will usually pause."

[46:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "High Retention = More Views"
NÚMEROS: Acima do benchmark
VISUAL: Exemplo mostrando vídeo com retention acima do baseline do canal resultando em mais views porque YouTube empurra para mais pessoas
[46:55]
"So, when your retention is high, and this is a benchmark of what this channel usually does for retention, they're above their current benchmark. And then you notice that their views are higher as a result. And that's because YouTube's like, hm, for this audience, this person actually is doing above their usual. So, we're just going to push it to more people like the audience because clearly this this video people are just like interested in retaining more of the video, watching longer."
[47:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Retention Actions & Lows"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Listas de ações que aumentam retention e coisas que causam quedas: sponsorships, CTAs estranhos, desvios do tópico principal
[47:35]
"So, these are retention actions. Once again, I'll drop the slide. Don't worry about taking a picture. Cool. retention lows, things like sponsorships, including like weird CTAs. That's where I mentioned like, 'Hey, and if you like this content, click the link.' But it's like, 'Don't do that. Make like a funny one.' Like integrated CTAs where it's like if you talk about a point, it's like, 'Yo, if you're if if you hate Mark Zuckerberg like me, like click the like button or something like that.' Like much more integrated CTAs."
[48:10]
"Retention lows as well. So, I blurred out the person because he was talking in this. I don't want to shame on him. Basically, he makes a video about like four ways to like clean a wound essentially. And then right here, for whatever reason, he thought it was a good idea to talk about his business."
[48:30]
"He was like, 'You know what? I'm just going to do a CTA.' And you could see it picks back up right here. And that's because he actually moved to the fourth point. Like people literally just didn't care. So that dropped his retention quite considerably because imagine if we moved this over here, you would have a way higher retention rate. And YouTube is like super analytical looking at that."

[49:35]
"I was I looked at this. I was like, I'm not working with you. And I fired that to be honest. I I I couldn't. I was like, 'No, you're in a you're in a really bad mindset to do this.' Like, you really really are. Like, this is not acceptable. Like, just look at that. Like, YouTube's never You've already destroyed your channel. I'd have to start all over again. that."

[50:05]

Basically, it's measuring your video against other videos and how well it's doing compared to videos, how it's grouping it and the certain time, things like that. It's not always available, but usually it is."
[50:30]
"So then you could say actually when I get around like the 80% mark I have a drop off. So it's like probably this video is doing a call to action there to be honest. Retention mistakes, you know, you could screenshot this later."
[50:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Titles & Thumbnails Tell a Story Together"

[51:15]
"So let's let's go over an example. Right? If you view these separately, they're actually not very interesting. But when you view them together, you're like, 'Oh, why is this color actually not yellow?' Right? Or it's kind of like known in the YouTube space. If you don't like edit your thumbnail super crazy, some shit's going down."
[51:40]
"So, it's like it's time for YouTube to step in. Like on its own, it doesn't really make sense. But then he has like a thumbnail like that and his audience is like, 'Oh crap, it's like one of not one of the stylized thumbnails. What's happening?'"
[52:00]
"Same thing here. Milky Way, super massive black hole. It's like, 'Cool.' It's like, 'Really? How long did they take this picture?' It's like, 'Oh, okay. They tell a story together.'"
[52:15]
"So the main question to always ask yourself is like does it have kind of that quality of having a video that only gets answered by you? A lot of people just make videos and anybody else can make that same video. It's like how is it very unique to you? That's where going from TAM to niche to focus really helps you because that focus is where people can only come to you for that answer."

[53:10]
"So when you have like a weight, so we're going to play off emotions. Everybody in this room is really good marketer, so this is going to come much more natural, so I'm going to blaze through this. You have a weight. This is like anticipation, right? You have joy. Cool. They're happy. You have trust. Great. Like authority building, blah blah blah blah."
[53:35]
"You have anger, which is like what's happening? Oh my god, doom and doom and gloom type of stuff. You have disgust, like, oh, I can't believe that's happening. you know, shit's coming out of a girl's ear, the horse video, things like that. Cool. You have fear, fear-mongering, you know, this is really apparent in America, you know. So, it's like, cool. Then you have sadness, you know, people being sad, you know, having to speak their exploits because they're sorry and publicly posted to YouTube. It's kind of funny sometimes."
[54:15]
"But that's basically like the heart emotion of what I go through for thinking about thumbnails. So, just pick one from the heart and then you have an emotional hook to your thumbnail already."

Um, he has a really good book called YouTube Formula. I recommend it for sure. He took a lot of the guesswork out of like thinking about formats for thumbnails."
[55:00]
"So, once again, I'll drop this inside, but this just has like I'm not going to try to reinvent the wheel. I use this as inspiration when I start thumbnail ideation as well, but there's different formats to go through when you're thinking about pairing it with an emotion."
[55:20]
"So, when you're thinking about a thumbnail, last thing I'll kind of mention is just think about the emotion that the thumbnail is having, what it is in relation to your audience. So, like you're probably not making thumbnails like Mr. Beast because it'd be really weird and I'd laugh at you and I'd put you in my next presentation as an example of what not to do."
[55:45]
"And then brand, like, you know, when it's from a certain creator, it's like I follow Hormosi, so it's like, you know, it's like kind of back in the day it's like purple and yellow text, right? It's like, oh yeah, that's like a Hormosi video. It's like really give a little bit of consideration to how you're branding your thumbnails as well. And that could be like your reaction phase, things like that."
[56:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Clickbait vs Legit Bait Spectrum"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Espectro mostrando balance entre clickbait puro (alto drop-off) e conteúdo relevante mas atraente
[56:15]
"Then we talk about legit bait, clickbait. You know, back in the day, it was like really clickbait worthy, but now the algorithm looks at retention too much. So, you don't want really want to be on that scale of super clickbay because you'll have a super high drop off rate like that terrible video I showed you. You don't want to be in that zone. So, I would say you just stray closer to the clickbait in a way, but you still keep it pretty relevant."
[56:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Scroll Whiplash Concept"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de fazer alguém scrollar ALÉM do seu vídeo e depois voltar - competindo contra múltiplas opções
[56:55]
"Now, my favorite thing is a term. It's called scroll whiplash. So, we're all scrolling on our phones. A lot of people say like, 'Oh, you want them to stop at your thumbnail.' I'm like, 'I want something a little better.' I want you to scroll past my video and be like, 'Oh, What actually was that?' And you come back down."
[57:20]
"I'm like, 'That to me is when you won the game because you competed against the next thumbnail or thumbnails and they're like, 'Oh I'm actually going to scroll back up to that video.' Not just stop because it's like, oh yeah, it's very easy to make people stop, but to go back and have your thumbnail like be thought about after they were already presented with multiple options."
[57:50]
"I feel like there's like a Brandon Carter girl joke about that. But anyway, one quick note about scrolling on YouTube, by the way, is that you have autoplay to worry about. So, I believe thumbnails may potentially go away for autoplay."
[58:10]
"So it's very important that in the beginning of the video you're just like really showcasing you know what but it's harder because some you're not going to like do Mr. Beast text in all your videos but you just want to make it very clear because captions will be automatically turned on."
[58:30]
"So just make sure like you know if if your captions are there automatically turned on. Just make sure like you know it's like correct and it's not saying a curse word or something weird in the beginning so people are not drawn away."
[58:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Google Trust But Verify - Video Analysis"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Exemplo de análise automática do Google de vídeo do Jeremy, mostrando como YouTube escaneia todo conteúdo visual
[58:55]
"Just to keep in mind, I think Jeremy talked about this the other day, uh, last mastermind that Google will trust but verify everything you're doing. So, this is one Jeremy's thumbnails. So, it's just like, you know, it's going through. I'm going to blaze through this because we already talked about it one time, but it's essentially just going through analyzing the thumbnail."
[59:20]
"The really key thing to know here is that YouTube is doing this for your whole entire video. So, I'm just showing you a thumbnail, but really what the algorithm is doing, it's just going through the whole video. Like, uh, an example is they use one of Mr. beast videos to showcase their product and it like tells you what what store they're in like he's like a Caucasian male. It's like it's telling you everything about it and then feeding it into the algorithm to kind of in a way like build a lookalike audience I guess of the ideal viewer for that type of video."
[1:00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Important Thumbnail & Title Tips"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Lista de dicas práticas (texto pequeno na tela para referência posterior)
[1:00:05]
"Important thumbnails tip that's really small on screen that's definitely not designed like that. But once again I'll drop the presentation. It's all good. Important title tips drop the presentation."
[1:00:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Thumbnail Switching Strategy"
NÚMEROS: Primeira hora ou primeiro dia, 7 dias, evergreen
VISUAL: Sistema de teste A/B de thumbnails: Opção A (safe), Opção B (variation), Last Resort (mais clickbait)
[1:00:25]
"So, one last thing, if you're not doing this next thing, like this is how we ended up growing Dan's channel quite a bit. Um, it's not really highly leveraged is just thumbnail switching. Like a lot of people think it's like I release my video and that's it."
[1:00:45]
"Now, shorts, by the way, shorts do have a thumbnail if you upload via mobile, not by desktop. So, depending on the velocity of views, it could be the first hour or the first day. you kind of just define a range so you're always testing the right thing but you're switching in and out options."
[1:01:10]
"If you want me to go super in-depth in this just like ask me and I can. It's very hard to talk about this concept very quickly. Just know that basically what we're doing is we're having option A, B, and the last resort that the last resort is much more on the clickbait side than anything else. And then A and B is like kind of more safer choices."
[1:01:35]
"So like this is a channel I help manage. Option A, she's like making a horror game in PowerPoint. Option A, option B, and then last resort. So you kind of see how different the concept actually becomes for last resort and how different option B is from option A essentially."
[1:02:00]
"Then you have 7-day thumbnail switching. So once it goes to the core audience of the algorithm, then your core audience is kind of satisfied. Like imagine when you scroll your subscription feed, like the chances of you seeing a video that was recommended 7 days ago is way less. So you optimize for different things on thumbnail switching."
[1:02:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Thumbnail Testing Tools"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Duas opções: TubeBuddy (ferramenta paga para testes automáticos) e YouTube's Test & Compare (feature beta oficial)
[1:02:30]
"So, two tools that do this really good. Uh, TubeBuddy, if you like, it's much worth it for the next thing I'll talk about, but TubeBuddy is like pretty inexpensive and they do thumbnail testing and then you just pick whichever one's the winner. They kind of do it on autopilot."
[1:02:50]
"Or if for whatever reason you see this in your dashboard, this is YouTube's long awaited test and compare thumbnails. However, this doesn't work for old videos. It only works for videos being released in the moment. But, this is like super beta feature. Everybody's asked for it for like seven years and we're finally seeing it being rolled out to beta beta people."
[1:03:20]
"And then evergreen thumbnail switching. This one is really important. This is what we actually do with Dan and it takes a lot of work if you have over 2,000 videos like we did, but essentially you're switching the thumbnail for all the old videos for just better performance."
[1:03:45]
"So like you have to have a VA or designer just switch thumbnails. Another good thing is like let's say you released a video that you knew was going to be a banger. As time goes on, you could test a different thumbnail for that banger video. if you're going to remake that banger video or you do more videos like that in the future."
[1:04:10]
"So, you could test on old content. You don't need to like test on the new content and potentially up your views."
[1:04:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Vital Metrics for YouTube Channels"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Monitor de "sinais vitais" do canal: Real-time (pulse), Views (circulation), AVD (absorption), CTR (interest), Retention (temperature)
[1:04:30]
"So, going to go over the viral the vital metrics that I look at when measuring a YouTube channel. Like these are in order of importance. I kind of think about this as like, you know, next to the hospital beds they have that little vital monitor. This is kind of how I think about it for like YouTube channels."
[1:04:55]
"So, number one thing is subscribers. No, I'm just kidding. This is like pure ego. Subscribers like don't matter whatsoever."
[1:05:10]
"So first thing that I look at is real time. This is kind of like the pulse on the vital monitor. So like what's actually happening in real time on the channel? How is it living and breathing? What's the pulse? What is the content that's working right now?"
[1:05:30]
"Next thing we have is views. So I kind of think about like the circulation. How is it being circulated all throughout the stream of consciousness for the audience, right? And there's many different ways to look at the views and what happens."
[1:05:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Views Are Not Created Equal"
NÚMEROS: 30 dias
VISUAL: Diferentes padrões de performance de vídeo: acima do baseline inicial, underperforming depois pega, flatline, consistente abaixo
[1:05:55]
"It's kind of hard if you're in the back because there's like a gray area, but you could see views are not created equal. Sometimes you're way above baseline in the beginning. Sometimes videos take off later, right? It's like underperforming for 30 days. Probably do a thumbnail switch and then you propel it. Same thing as like videos just pick up and then kind of flatline again or just below performance."
[1:06:25]
"So always just know like you could always optimize these things with thumbnails, things like that. And you and you have the range of how well your video is doing."
[1:06:40]
"Then you have average view duration, average percentage viewed. This is like how much people are absorbing your content. So once again, that's how you view it. And it's just like key moments and audience retention, which we talked a little bit about already."
[1:07:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Click-Through Rate Deep Dive"
NÚMEROS: 9.9% (browse), 2.6% (suggested)
VISUAL: Importância de analisar CTR por traffic source ao invés de agregado, exemplo mostrando performance diferente entre browse vs suggested
[1:07:05]
"Then you have click-through rate. Once again, very important thing for click-through rate. Of course, you're measuring kind of how well it's doing over time. However, clickthrough rate, where most people go wrong, is that clickthrough rate, how YouTube displays it at first, is an aggregate measurement."
[1:07:30]
"So, it's looking at all the different algorithms for YouTube and traffic sources and then it's averaging out the clickthrough rate. But if you notice my suggested versus browse browse is at 9.9. So, I'm like killing it, bro. And then suggested videos is 2.6. So, I'm like, 'Oh we're actually like not appeasing to both algorithms.'"
[1:08:00]
"So, it's like most people will just look at the top number and potentially do a change. always dig into the traffic channel because what might be happening for click-through rate is just because click-through rate dips doesn't mean it's actually bad. What's happening is that it's going to a more broader audience."
[1:08:25]
"So, of course, naturally, your clickthrough rate is going to drop because it's going to a broader audience. So, a lot of creators start to freak out. They're like, 'Oh my god.' And the thing it's telling me that my my clickthrough rate's down, it's like, 'It's okay, bro. Everything's going to be fine. Just look at how much impressions are actually going up.'"
[1:08:50]
"Because in turn, you're getting a lot of views. So, like YouTube doesn't make it super easy, but they show you like how many views and it's going up. But because clickthrough rate is down, people are like, 'But my clickthrough rate is down.' I'm like, 'Yeah, but your views are up.' Views are the amalgamation of all of the data. That's the final outcome. So, the final outcome is good. Don't change things."
[1:09:20]
"Like, somebody who who has a YouTube channel right now, actually, cool. So, you know how like on the when you run a YouTube channel, it'll rank all the videos that you have and it'll say, 'Oh, you released a one out of 10 or a seven out of 10?' It's like disastrous for your ego. But when you get a one out of 10, you're like, 'Oh yeah, yeah. I got that shit.' Right."
[1:09:50]
"So for one out of 10, I had She was texting me and I was She was like, 'I have a one out of 10, but my clickthrough rates down.' I'm like, 'Don't change a thing. You have a one out of 10. Do not do anything.'"
[1:10:10]
"Right on the homepage as soon as you log into the studio, it has like a little title thing and it just says or you could just open the mobile app, it'll tell you. I'll show you later. Yeah."
[1:10:25]
"So I was like, 'Don't touch anything.' And then 6 hours later, the video is still doing very well and YouTube like gives you a little popup and it's like, 'Hey, we pushed your video to more people.' So your clickthrough rate's down. I was like, 'See, I told you. Don't worry.'"
[1:10:50]
"Then we take the temperature, which is essentially the retention. How hot is the video doing? Are people watching 100% of it? Is it cold as And people nobody's watching it. And then obviously YouTube gives you analytics on that as well."
[1:11:10]
"Key thing to measure once again, as I said before, you're measuring average view percentage based on traffic channel. That's what you care about the most. You don't care about the algamated number because it's not very useful to you. People who search obviously are going to have a higher average view percentage than people who just got pushed it brand new. But you're going to want to optimize and make certain decisions based off of what is happening."
[1:11:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Diagnostic Metrics"
NÚMEROS: 80%+ (YouTube Love threshold)
VISUAL: Métricas específicas adicionais: YouTube Love (funnel de recomendação), Short performance (viewed vs swiped), New viewer attraction, Video Coliseum (comparação entre vídeos)
[1:11:50]
"Like I said in the beginning, it just depends. Now, these are other metrics. These are like specific diagnostics that I go into. So like if I have the vital monitor, I'm like, 'Cool, at a glance, I know what's happening, but there's still something wrong in why you're in the hospital.'"
[1:12:15]
"So I call this one YouTube love. This is actually probably it's going to be hard to see on screen. show everything, but YouTube has like a little funnel metric that they show you and then it shows you how much YouTube's recommending your content."
[1:12:35]
"So, this is essentially telling you how much does the algorithm love your baby video. And then what it's doing is when you usually see, remember I was talking earlier about the algorithm. When you see browse and suggested videos on top in terms of how people are finding this video, it's because YouTube is most likely pushing it to that audience."
[1:13:00]
"Your channel is in a really good spot if that number is above 80%. If that you're you're you're definitely growing."
[1:13:15]
"So, how good is your YouTube short? Basically, there's a new metric that got introduced is like viewed versus swiped away. So, it's just like, you know, in your profile. It's like how many how many matches did you get? That's essentially what YouTube is telling you."
[1:13:40]
"Next thing that we're going to talk about is like are you actually attracting new viewers that like your stuff? It's like a an actual way to quantify is your video actually appealing to new audiences. So, we just YouTube gives you a metric. It's called average views per viewer. You're dividing total views by unique viewers."
[1:14:10]
"Reason it's important for unique viewers is because those are people who have never seen your content before. They're not a returning viewer. So, they're unique to that first instance of viewing your content. And then we're dividing the amount of total views to just get that number."
[1:14:35]
"So, once again, YouTube makes it a little easier, too. They'll have that dashboard. I like to be a little more data because here I'm actually measuring it by months. Sorry, in this case, weeks. So, I'm seeing like is what we're doing week to week actually growing the audience or are we just kind of not not hitting the right vein with the audience?"
[1:15:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Video Coliseum - Compare Videos"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Método de comparar múltiplos vídeos usando mesma métrica, identificar top e bottom performers, NÃO comparar formatos diferentes (podcast vs short)
[1:15:05]
"Now, I call this the video coliseum. This is actually how you pit your videos against each other. So, what you're doing is you're comparing a lot of stuff against each other using the same metric. Big asterisk to this one. If you have videos that are like, let's say a podcast versus a shirt, a short, you do not compare that. That is not the same thing."
[1:15:35]
"They have they have a different they have a different uh view rate and everything. So you want to like group them together and then you could see top performers and also just look at the bottom performers and just like probably never make that content again to be honest. Like just like oh well that didn't work."
[1:16:00]
"Keep in mind you could do this for different things. things we I was showing you by views, but the really cool one that I like to do is new viewers who returned because that shows you a new unique viewer who then came back to your channel, which YouTube likes to see a lot of that because they're like, 'Oh we found the right correlation of a person coming back to your channel.'"
[1:16:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quickfire To-Dos"
NÚMEROS: 8 minutos, 2.5 minutos (intervalo para midroll ads)
VISUAL: Lista de ações imediatas: inserir midroll ads manualmente, authority signals, spike ad angles, push winners harder, community posts, email strategy
[1:16:35]
"So, this is going to get very tactical really fast. We're going to do quickfire to-dos. So, immediately boost to AdSense revenue. This is like free money. This is the first thing that I do when I go into channels. For every video that you have above 8 minutes, you manually insert midroll ads around every two and a half minutes."
[1:17:00]
"YouTube automatically inserts them, but they don't do the best job. Just because you insert an ad, like a midroll ad, you're just allowing a spot for YouTube to put an ad there. It doesn't mean that somebody like you're going to interrupt somebody. They're just give you're just giving YouTube the option."
[1:17:25]
"So, if you think about it, if you give more options to advertisers to advertise on your video, you're probably going to make more money and so will YouTube. So, they like that."
[1:17:40]
"The other thing is authority signal. So just using like if you put doctor in your channel name, doctor says, lawyer explains, you know, logo and brand associations, spike ad angles. This one we used at Dan's, this is probably the number one thing that we did with Dan."
[1:18:00]
"Uh we would look at the retention graph and anytime there was a spike, we would cut it out and make an ad just from that specific piece that he was talking about. This now I mean obviously you have shorts now. Back then when I grew Dan's channel, I didn't have shorts. It was just like give it to the Instagram team and Facebook and to Jeremy basically."
[1:18:30]
"Uh push what works harder. So I call this the Brian compromise. I wish Brian was here uh for YouTube ads. So him and I have a a rival debate on whether you should run ads on an organic channel or not. The compromise that I have is like if end of the day you're here to make money."
[1:18:55]
"If that video is doing very well and getting you book calls or sales, just run a duplicate for discovery ads. Don't don't like touch the real organic one. Just run a duplicate. It's okay. And just pay close attention to CTR."
[1:19:15]
"Other thing is community post. I think someone actually in the inner circle chat posted a community post that they saw from Alex Armosi. He's like, 'Bro, I'm not even subscribed to him. How the did this happen?' It's because community posts get pushed out to an ideal audience as well."
[1:19:40]
"So, it's your subscriber base mostly, but then it also gets pushed out to an audience. So, one of our clients, you know, Corey, he did like a Instagram shoutout post, but on community post, and he ended up filling up his calendar that we had to like stop running ads because he got really booked up."
[1:20:10]
"Uh emailing videos to your list. I see this one a lot. So, YouTube's external traffic algorithm that I talked about earlier, they don't really know what what's happening. So, they think like foul play is going on. So, what's what you do instead is just show a picture of the thumbnail and send people to the video tab and people will just make the association."
[1:20:40]
"You probably have some drop off, but I rather the algorithm see that people are just like coming to your channel and then watching your latest video than a unknown viewer coming into a video where YouTube can't track."
[1:21:00]
"Wait, you you're saying when the previous slide you do an email, you're saying just take a screenshot with the thumbnail image as a hyperlink email and send them to the YouTube video. Not to the YouTube video, to the video tab. Like just send them to the channel. Don't send them directly to the video. Yeah. So like you when you on YouTube like you go to a creator and they have like a video tab. It's like a link on the desktop."
[1:21:35]
"And why do you not want them to go straight to the Yeah. So, YouTube when it views something coming from email, it doesn't know that it came from email. So, what's happening is like, oh, there's this random person that just popped up on this video. And that's what a lot of bot farms do."
[1:22:00]
"So, what ends up happening is that YouTube external algorithm gets triggered to view the video versus a suggestion or homepage algorithm. So when they're when they click into the YouTube app and then they go into your channel and click the video, what YouTube says is like, oh, this person's on this person's channel and then they're watching a video. So this algorithm is used instead."
[1:22:30]
"It's use it's using the recommendation or homepage algorithm more than it is the external algorithm because once again, the external algorithm doesn't really do much. It's just like there on the bottom tier. Yep. Did that clear it up? Cool."
[1:22:50]
"So once again, just redo your top video, similar thumbnail, just twist. look at the retention dips for that top video and just see how you can improve upon it. AVD editing, average view duration, just look at the average view duration for content very similar. Once again, shorts different than podcasts and just edit one minute above."
[1:23:20]
"Once again, this is all about just making 1% improvements over time. So, just increase the the quality a little bit and just like this is how I actually run the production teams for editors. I will just look at the video and be like just edit to four minutes and I will critique the first four minutes. I don't give a about the rest of the video to be honest because I'm just trying to increase it little by little."

[1:25:45]
"So like all the students back in the day with Dan is that we would have we would require them to watch 10 of Dan's videos a day. Like we were like that's part of your homework is to be indoctrinated into this cult. So you need to watch 10 videos a day."
[1:26:10]
"And now of course not everybody did it, but it's a really good way for YouTube to know that an audience just keeps coming back and back to those videos. Or if you have like daily meditations, like Dan has three daily meditations that he would require the students to do."
[1:26:35]
"It's pretty wild. Like he's definitely NLPing the out of everyone. It's like in the morning in the in the midday and then at night you just leave it to loop all night and then it's like you just see that one video has tons of watch time because people just go to sleep for like eight hours and it's just looping."

[1:27:05]

[1:27:25]
"So VPH is views per hour. It'll tell you which channels like you know how I was talking about real time. This is showing you a little bit of what like Harosi in this case what's working for his channel real time."
[1:27:45]
"And then once again you could sometimes on desktop you could scroll through videos and you could actually see the retention of other people's videos. This is like a super cool hack when I saw this. It was introduced a while ago, but it's like just go to other people's content and see, hey, what is working for them and how can I just duplicate that?"

[1:28:45]
"So, you could ask it like really cool stuff like I was going to San Francisco. Anybody watch AllIn podcast? There we go. Yes. I love y'all. I was like, 'Yo, this is dope as shit.'"

You watch videos from a creator who you're trying to like imitate in a way and then just ghost the algorithm for seven days and it's going to be like dying like a heroin addict to get you back."
[1:32:20]
